description: An Italian rice dish made by gradually cooking short-grain rice in stock until creamy. The texture comes from the rice starch rather than from heavy cream, though butter and cheese are often added at the end.
core_ingredients: Short-grain rice such as Arborio, Carnaroli, or Vialone Nano
preparation_steps: Sauté onion or shallot in fat.
Add wine if using and let it absorb.
Add hot stock gradually, stirring and allowing each addition to absorb before the next.
Finish with butter, cheese, and any cooked add-ins before serving immediately.

nutrition.fat: Low to moderate, but higher with butter and cheese

health_advice.benefits: Can be satisfying and versatile, with room to include vegetables or seafood.
health_advice.risks: Rich versions can become high in sodium, butter, and cheese.
health_advice.healthy_tip: Add vegetables and use moderate amounts of butter and cheese for a more balanced dish.

dietary_notes.note: Naturally gluten-free when prepared with gluten-free stock and add-ins. Vegan versions are possible without butter and cheese.

allergens: Dairy if butter or cheese is used
